title: Security Council extends critical stabilisation force in Syria
content: The 15-member Security Council unanimously adopted a draft resolution on Thursday renewing the mandate of the UN Disengagement Observer Force (UNDOF) for six months.
snippet: The 15-member Security Council unanimously adopted a draft resolution on Thursday renewing the mandate of the UN Disengagement Observer Force (UNDOF) for six months.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Peace and Security

title: From Lebanon to the Strait of Hormuz, a Middle East hanging on fragile peace talks
content: Since Tuesday, United Nations peacekeepers have detected no airstrikes or new missile fire in southern Lebanon. The lull remains fragile, however, as Israeli drones continue to fly over the area and military operations continue on the ground.

title: UN envoy warns of growing risks in El Obeid as Sudan conflict drags on
content: The United Nations Secretary-General’s Personal Envoy for Sudan voiced alarm on Wednesday over escalating violence in and around the city of El Obeid, warning that further military escalation could put thousands of civilians at risk and deepen Sudan’s already devastating humanitarian crisis.

title: Thousands trapped in southern Ukraine struggle to survive
content: Thousands of civilians trapped in frontline communities in southern Ukraine are facing a deepening humanitarian crisis as access to food, medical care and evacuation routes continues to shrink, the UN Human Rights Monitoring Mission in the country, HMRRU, warned on Wednesday.

sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Peace and Security

title: Central African Republic peace gains must be preserved: UN envoy
content: The Central African Republic (CAR) “has made remarkable and tangible progress towards lasting peace and security” in recent years, but security remains fragile in border areas, including with war-torn Sudan, UN Special Representative Valentine Rugwabiza told the Security Council on Tuesday.

title: Sudan: Drone attacks endanger civilians and shut down critical services in El Obeid
content: Escalating violence in and around the Sudanese city of El Obeid is putting civilians at increased risk and disrupting essential services, the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Monday.
snippet: Escalating violence in and around the Sudanese city of El Obeid is putting civilians at increased risk and disrupting essential services, the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Monday.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Peace and Security

title: Afghanistan in crisis: Drought, malnutrition and a worsening humanitarian situation
content: Imagine being one of a family of nine and sitting down to a meal of potato peelings and other scraps, boiled up into a soup. This is the harsh reality for many of Afghanistan’s most vulnerable families, forced on them by climate change and drought, widespread malnutrition and increasing restrictions on women, since the Taliban overran Kabul in 2021.
snippet: Imagine being one of a family of nine and sitting down to a meal of potato peelings and other scraps, boiled up into a soup. This is the harsh reality for many of Afghanistan’s most vulnerable
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Peace and Security

title: Sudan: Security Council warns of mass atrocity risk in El Obeid
content: The UN Security Council has expressed alarm over reports of substantial military reinforcements by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) around El Obeid in Sudan, warning of the risk of a potential ground offensive on the city.

sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Peace and Security

title: Libya’s political process regains momentum, but window for action is narrowing, UN envoy warns
content: Libya has been mired in political dysfunction since the collapse of Muammar Gaddafi’s regime in 2011, which shattered State institutions and triggered recurring struggles over legitimacy and power.
snippet: Libya has been mired in political dysfunction since the collapse of Muammar Gaddafi’s regime in 2011, which shattered State institutions and triggered recurring struggles over legitimacy and power.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Peace and Security

title: ‘Stop this madness’: Rights chief warns of impending atrocities as militia closes in on El Obeid, Sudan
content: The UN’s top human rights official has issued an urgent warning that an imminent offensive against El Obeid, the capital of Sudan’s North Kordofan state, carries the risk of serious international crimes and threatens to deepen an already catastrophic humanitarian crisis.
